17	package v1beta1
18	
19	import (
20		"fmt"
21		"strings"
22	
23		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
24	
25		"github.com/openshift/library-go/pkg/operator/v1helpers"

28		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common/plugins"
29	)
30	
31	// ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec defines the desired state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig
32	type ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec struct {
33		// LogVerbosity is the log level for the pod placement components.
34		// Valid values are: "Normal", "Debug", "Trace", "TraceAll".
35		// Defaults to "Normal".
36		// +optional
37		// +kubebuilder:default=Normal
38		LogVerbosity common.LogVerbosityLevel `json:"logVerbosity,omitempty"`
39	
40		// NamespaceSelector selects the namespaces where the pod placement operand can process the nodeAffinity
41		// of the pods. If left empty, all the namespaces are considered.
42		// The default sample allows to exclude all the namespaces where the
43		// label "multiarch.openshift.io/exclude-pod-placement" exists.
44		// +optional
45		NamespaceSelector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:"namespaceSelector,omitempty"`
46	
47		// Plugins defines the configurable plugins for this component.
48		// This field is optional and will be omitted from the output if not set.
49		// +optional
50		Plugins *plugins.Plugins `json:"plugins,omitempty"`
51	
52		// FallbackArchitecture defines the architecture to use if the image inspector cannot determine the image's architecture.
53		// If configured, the PodPlacementController will set the node affinity of the pod to the fallback architecture
54		// if the image inspector cannot determine the image's architecture.
55		// +optional
56		// +kubebuilder:default=""
57		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=arm64;amd64;ppc64le;s390x;""
58		FallbackArchitecture string `json:"fallbackArchitecture,omitempty"`
59	}
60	
61	// ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus defines the observed state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig
62	type ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus struct {
63		// Conditions represents the latest available observations of a ClusterPodPlacementConfig's current state.
64		Conditions []metav1.Condition `json:"conditions,omitempty"`
65	
66		// The following fields are used to derive the conditions. They are not exposed to the user.
67		//nolint:revive // controller-gen requires json tags on all fields, even unexported ones
68		available                                bool `json:"-"`
69		progressing                              bool `json:"-"`
70		degraded                                 bool `json:"-"`
71		deprovisioning                           bool `json:"-"`
72		podPlacementControllerNotReady           bool `json:"-"`
73		podPlacementWebhookNotReady              bool `json:"-"`
74		mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable bool `json:"-"`
75		canDeployMutatingWebhook                 bool `json:"-"`
76	}
77	
78	func (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsReady() bool {
79		return s.available
80	}
81	
82	func (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsProgressing() bool {
83		return s.progressing
84	}
85	
86	func (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsDegraded() bool {
87		return s.degraded
88	}
89	
90	func (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsDeprovisioning() bool {
91		return s.deprovisioning
92	}
93	
94	func (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsPodPlacementControllerNotReady() bool {
95		return s.podPlacementControllerNotReady
96	}
97	
98	func (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsPodPlacementWebhookNotReady() bool {
99		return s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady
100	}
101	
102	func (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsMutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable() bool {
103		return s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable
104	}
105	
106	func (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) CanDeployMutatingWebhook() bool {
107		return s.canDeployMutatingWebhook
108	}
109	
110	// Build sets the conditions in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig object.
111	// The build Conditions are:
112	//   - Degraded: if some components are not available (no replicas) and the object is not deprovisioning
113	//   - Deprovisioning: if the object is being deleted
114	//   - MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable: if the mutating webhook configuration does not exist
115	//   - PodPlacementControllerNotReady: if the pod placement controller is not available or up-to-date
116	//   - PodPlacementWebhookNotReady: if the pod placement webhook is not available or up-to-date
117	//   - Progressing: if the object is not deprovisioning and some of the components are not up-to-date.
118	//   - Available: if all the components are available to serve the requests and reconcile node affinities (at least one replica).
119	func (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) Build(
120		podPlacementControllerAvailable, podPlacementWebhookAvailable,
121		podPlacementControllerUpToDate, podPlacementWebhookUpToDate,
122		mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable,
123		deprovisioning bool) {
124		s.deprovisioning = deprovisioning
125		// tracks existence of the mutating webhook configuration
126		s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable = !mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable
127		// tracks the availability of the pod placement controller and webhook and if they are up to date
128		s.podPlacementControllerNotReady = !podPlacementControllerAvailable || !podPlacementControllerUpToDate
129		s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady = !podPlacementWebhookAvailable || !podPlacementWebhookUpToDate
130		// if all the components exist and have at least one replica ready
131		s.available = mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable && podPlacementWebhookAvailable && podPlacementControllerAvailable
132		// if some components are not available (no replicas)
133		s.degraded = !s.available && !s.deprovisioning // degraded will not track deprovisioning
134		// allow the deployment of the mutating webhook configuration if the pod placement controller and webhook are available
135		// (at least one replica)
136		s.canDeployMutatingWebhook = podPlacementWebhookAvailable && podPlacementControllerAvailable && !s.deprovisioning
137		s.progressing = (!podPlacementControllerUpToDate || !podPlacementWebhookUpToDate || !mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable) && !s.deprovisioning
138		s.buildConditions()
139	}
140	
141	func (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) buildConditions() {
142		if s.Conditions == nil {
143			s.Conditions = []metav1.Condition{}
144		}
145		reason := ""
146		if s.podPlacementControllerNotReady {
147			reason += PodPlacementControllerNotRolledOutType
148		}
149		if s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady {
150			reason += PodPlacementWebhookNotRolledOutType
151		}
152		if s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable {
153			reason += MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable
154		}
155		if reason == "" {
156			reason = AllComponentsReady
157		}
158		v1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
159			Type:    AvailableType,
160			Status:  conditionFromBool(s.available),
161			Reason:  reason,
162			Message: fmt.Sprintf(ReadyMsg, notFromBool(s.available), strings.TrimSpace(notFromBool(s.available))),
163		})
164		v1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
165			Type:    ProgressingType,
166			Status:  conditionFromBool(s.progressing),
167			Reason:  reason,
168			Message: fmt.Sprintf(ProgressingMsg, notFromBool(s.progressing)),
169		})
170		v1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
171			Type:    DegradedType,
172			Status:  conditionFromBool(s.degraded),
173			Reason:  fmt.Sprintf("%s%s", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(s.degraded)), DegradedType),
174			Message: fmt.Sprintf(DegradedMsg, notFromBool(s.degraded)),
175		})
176		deprovisinoingMessagePostfix := ""
177		if s.deprovisioning {
178			deprovisinoingMessagePostfix = PendingDeprovisioningMsg
179		}
180		v1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
181			Type:    DeprovisioningType,
182			Status:  conditionFromBool(s.deprovisioning),
183			Reason:  fmt.Sprintf("%s%s", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(s.deprovisioning)), DeprovisioningType),
184			Message: fmt.Sprintf(DeprovisioningMsg, notFromBool(s.deprovisioning), deprovisinoingMessagePostfix),
185		})
186		v1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
187			Type:    PodPlacementControllerNotRolledOutType,
188			Status:  conditionFromBool(s.podPlacementControllerNotReady),
189			Reason:  fmt.Sprintf("PodPlacementController%sReady", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(!s.podPlacementControllerNotReady))),
190			Message: fmt.Sprintf(PodPlacementControllerRolledOutMsg, notFromBool(!s.podPlacementControllerNotReady)),
191		})
192		v1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
193			Type:    PodPlacementWebhookNotRolledOutType,
194			Status:  conditionFromBool(s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady),
195			Reason:  fmt.Sprintf("PodPlacementWebhook%sReady", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(!s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady))),
196			Message: fmt.Sprintf(PodPlacementWebhookRolledOutMsg, notFromBool(!s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady)),
197		})
198		v1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
199			Type:    MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable,
200			Status:  conditionFromBool(s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable),
201			Reason:  reason,
202			Message: fmt.Sprintf(MutatingWebhookConfigurationReadyMsg, notFromBool(!s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable)),
203		})
204	}
205	
206	// ClusterPodPlacementConfig defines the configuration for the architecture aware pod placement operand.
207	// Users can only deploy a single object named "cluster".
208	// Creating the object enables the operand.
209	// +kubebuilder:object:root=true
210	// +kubebuilder:storageversion
211	// +kubebuilder:subresource:status
212	// +kubebuilder:resource:path=clusterpodplacementconfigs,scope=Cluster
213	// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Available,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Available")].status,type=string
214	// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Progressing,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Progressing")].status,type=string
215	// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Degraded,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Degraded")].status,type=string
216	// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Since,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Progressing")].lastTransitionTime,type=date
217	// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Status,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Available")].reason,type=string
218	type ClusterPodPlacementConfig struct {
219		metav1.TypeMeta   `json:",inline"`
220		metav1.ObjectMeta `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
221	
222		Spec   ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec   `json:"spec,omitempty"`
223		Status ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus `json:"status,omitempty"`
224	}
225	
226	func (c *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) PluginsEnabled(plugin common.Plugin) bool {
227		if c.Spec.Plugins != nil {
228			return c.Spec.Plugins.PluginEnabled(plugin)
229		}
230		return false
231	}
232	
233	//+kubebuilder:object:root=true
234	
235	// ClusterPodPlacementConfigList contains a list of ClusterPodPlacementConfig
236	type ClusterPodPlacementConfigList struct {
237		metav1.TypeMeta `json:",inline"`
238		metav1.ListMeta `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
239		Items           []ClusterPodPlacementConfig `json:"items"`
240	}
241	
242	func conditionFromBool(b bool) metav1.ConditionStatus {
243		if b {
244			return metav1.ConditionTrue
245		}
246		return metav1.ConditionFalse
247	}
248	
249	func notFromBool(b bool) string {
250		if b {
251			return ""
252		}
253		return "not "
254	}
255	
256	func trimAndCapitalize(s string) string {
257		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(s)
258		if len(trimmed) == 0 {
259			return trimmed
260		}
261		return strings.ToUpper(string(trimmed[0])) + trimmed[1:]
262	}
263

17	package v1alpha1
18	
19	import (
20		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/conversion"
21	
22		multiarchv1beta1 "github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1"
23	)
24	
25	const FallbackArchAnnotation = "multiarch.openshift.io/fallback-architecture"
26	
27	// ConvertTo converts this ClusterPodPlacementConfig to the Hub version v1beta1.
28	func (src *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) ConvertTo(dstRaw conversion.Hub) error {
29		dst := dstRaw.(*multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig)
30	
31		// ObjectMeta
32		dst.ObjectMeta = src.ObjectMeta
33	
34		// Spec
35		dst.Spec.LogVerbosity = src.Spec.LogVerbosity
36		dst.Spec.NamespaceSelector = src.Spec.NamespaceSelector
37		// Restore FallbackArchitecture from the annotation, if present.
38		// This ensures the value is preserved across API version conversions.
39		if arch, ok := src.Annotations[FallbackArchAnnotation]; ok {
40			dst.Spec.FallbackArchitecture = arch
41		}
42	
43		// Status
44		dst.Status.Conditions = src.Status.Conditions
45	
46		// +kubebuilder:docs-gen:collapse=rote conversion
47		return nil
48	}
49	
50	/*
51	ConvertFrom is expected to modify its receiver to contain the converted object.
52	Most of the conversion is straightforward copying, except for converting our changed field.
53	*/
54	
55	// ConvertFrom converts from the Hub version (v1beta1) to this.
56	func (dst *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) ConvertFrom(srcRaw conversion.Hub) error {
57		src := srcRaw.(*multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig)
58	
59		// ObjectMeta
60		dst.ObjectMeta = src.ObjectMeta
61		// v1alpha1 does not have the FallbackArchitecture field.
62		// Preserve the value in an annotation to avoid data loss during
63		// v1beta1 -> v1alpha1 -> v1beta1 round-trip conversions.
64		if dst.Annotations == nil {
65			dst.Annotations = make(map[string]string)
66		}
67		if src.Spec.FallbackArchitecture != "" {
68			dst.Annotations[FallbackArchAnnotation] = src.Spec.FallbackArchitecture
69		} else {
70			delete(dst.Annotations, FallbackArchAnnotation)
71		}
72	
73		// Spec
74		dst.Spec.LogVerbosity = src.Spec.LogVerbosity
75		dst.Spec.NamespaceSelector = src.Spec.NamespaceSelector
76	
77		// Status
78		dst.Status.Conditions = src.Status.Conditions
79	
80		if dst.Annotations == nil {
81			dst.Annotations = make(map[string]string)
82		}
83	
84		return nil
85	}

1	package internal
2	
3	import (
4		"sync"
5	
6		multiarchv1beta1 "github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1"
7	)
8	
9	var (
10		mu                        sync.RWMutex
11		clusterPodPlacementConfig *multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig
12	)
13	
14	func StoreClusterPodPlacementConfig(config *multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig) {
15		mu.Lock()
16		defer mu.Unlock()
17		clusterPodPlacementConfig = config.DeepCopy()
18	}
19	
20	func DeleteClusterPodPlacementConfig() {
21		mu.Lock()
22		defer mu.Unlock()
23		clusterPodPlacementConfig = nil
24	}
25	
26	func GetClusterPodPlacementConfig() *multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig {
27		mu.RLock()
28		defer mu.RUnlock()
29		return clusterPodPlacementConfig
30	}

text: Now let me check how `maxRetries()` is called in the reconciler and where the CPPC config is accessed there.

content:

16	
17	package podplacement
18	
19	import (
20		"context"
21		"fmt"
22		runtime2 "runtime"
23		"sort"
24		"time"
25	
26		corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
27		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
28		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
29		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"
30		"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
31		"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record"
32		ctrl "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime"
33		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
34		ctrl2 "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/controller"
35		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/handler"
36		ctrllog "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log"
37		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/reconcile"
38	
39		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common"
40		multiarchv1beta1 "github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1"
41		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacement/metrics"
42		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/informers/clusterpodplacementconfig"
43		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils"
44	)
45	
46	// PodReconciler reconciles a Pod object
47	type PodReconciler struct {
48		client.Client
49		APIReader client.Reader
50		Scheme    *runtime.Scheme
51		ClientSet *kubernetes.Clientset
52		Recorder  record.EventRecorder
53	}
54	
55	// Note: The operand (pod-placement-controller) RBAC is defined programmatically in
56	// podplacement_objects.go buildClusterRoleController(). Do NOT add operand-specific
57	// RBAC markers here — kubebuilder merges all markers into the manager's ClusterRole,
58	// which would over-provision the operator SA with permissions only the operand needs.
59	
60	// Reconcile is part of the main kubernetes reconciliation loop which aims to
61	// move the current state of the cluster closer to the desired state.
62	// the Pod object against the actual cluster state, and then
63	// perform operations to make the cluster state reflect the state specified by
64	// the user.
65	//
66	// For more details, check Reconcile and its Result here:
67	// - https://pkg.go.dev/sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime@v0.14.1/pkg/reconcile
68	// Reconcile has to watch the pod object if it has the scheduling gate with name SchedulingGateName,
69	// inspect the images in the pod spec, update the nodeAffinity accordingly and remove the scheduling gate.
70	func (r *PodReconciler) Reconcile(ctx context.Context, req ctrl.Request) (ctrl.Result, error) {
71		// Lazy initialization of the metrics to support concurrent reconciles
72		metrics.InitPodPlacementControllerMetrics()
73		now := time.Now()
74		defer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToProcessPod)
75		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)
76	
77		pod := newPod(&corev1.Pod{}, ctx, r.Recorder)
78	
79		if err := r.Get(ctx, req.NamespacedName, pod.PodObject()); err != nil {
80			log.V(2).Info("Unable to fetch pod", "error", err)
81			return ctrl.Result{}, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
82		}
83		// Pods without the scheduling gate should be ignored.
84		if !pod.HasSchedulingGate() {
85			log.V(2).Info("Pod does not have the scheduling gate. Ignoring...")
86			return ctrl.Result{}, nil
87		}
88		metrics.ProcessedPodsCtrl.Inc()
89		defer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToProcessGatedPod)
90		r.processPod(ctx, pod)
91		err := r.Update(ctx, pod.PodObject())
92		if err != nil {
93			log.Error(err, "Unable to update the pod")
94			pod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ArchitectureAwareSchedulingGateRemovalFailure, SchedulingGateRemovalFailureMsg)
95			return ctrl.Result{}, err
96		}
97		if !pod.HasSchedulingGate() {
98			// Only publish the event if the scheduling gate has been removed and the pod has been updated successfully.
99			pod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareSchedulingGateRemovalSuccess, SchedulingGateRemovalSuccessMsg)
100			metrics.GatedPodsGauge.Dec()
101		}
102		return ctrl.Result{}, nil
103	}
104	
105	func (r *PodReconciler) processPod(ctx context.Context, pod *Pod) {
106		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)
107		log.V(1).Info("Processing pod")
108	
109		cppc := clusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig()
110		// List existing PodPlacementConfigs in the same namespace
111		ppcList := &multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigList{}
112		if err := r.List(ctx, ppcList, client.InNamespace(pod.Namespace)); err != nil {
113			pod.handleError(err, "failed to list existing PodPlacementConfigs in namespace")
114			return
115		}
116		// The informer cache can lag behind the API server. If the cache shows no PPCs,
117		// verify with a direct API read to avoid a race where a just-created PPC is missed
118		// and the pod is permanently ungated without its preferred affinity.
119		if len(ppcList.Items) == 0 {
120			if err := r.APIReader.List(ctx, ppcList, client.InNamespace(pod.Namespace)); err != nil {
121				pod.handleError(err, "failed to list PodPlacementConfigs from API server")
122				return
123			}
124		}
125	
126		// Filter to only PPCs that match this pod's labels - do this once for efficiency
127		matchingPPCs := pod.filterMatchingPPCs(ppcList)
128	
129		if pod.shouldIgnorePod(cppc, matchingPPCs) {
130			log.V(3).Info("A pod with the scheduling gate should be ignored. Ignoring...")
131			// We can reach this branch when:
132			// - The pod has been gated but not processed before the operator changed configuration such that the pod should be ignored.
133			// - The pod has got some other changes in the admission chain from another webhook that makes it not suitable for processing anymore
134			//	(for example another actor set the nodeAffinity already for the kubernetes.io/arch label).
135			// In both cases, we should just remove the scheduling gate.
136			log.V(1).Info("Removing the scheduling gate from pod.")
137			pod.RemoveSchedulingGate()
138			pod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ArchitectureAwareGatedPodIgnored, ArchitectureAwareGatedPodIgnoredMsg)
139			return
140		}
141	
142		// Skip preferred affinity processing if the user has already configured architecture-related preferred affinity
143		// or if the reconcile loop has already applied the PPCs/CPPC (e.g., due to a retry or re-reconciliation)
144		if !pod.isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() {
145			r.applyMatchingPPCs(ctx, matchingPPCs, pod)
146	
147			if cppc != nil && cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {
148				pod.SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity(cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfigKind)
149			}
150		} else {
151			log.V(2).Info("Pod already has architecture-related preferred affinity. This could be user-defined or from a previous reconcile loop. Skipping PPC/CPPC preferred affinity processing.")
152			// Track that configs were skipped due to user-defined preferences
153			r.trackSkippedMatchingConfigs(ctx, pod, cppc, matchingPPCs)
154		}
155	
156		// Prepare the requirement for the node affinity.
157		psdl, err := r.pullSecretDataList(ctx, pod)
158		pod.handleError(err, "Unable to retrieve the image pull secret data for the pod.")
159		// If no error occurred when retrieving the image pull secret data, set the node affinity.
160		if err == nil {
161			_, err = pod.SetNodeAffinityArchRequirement(psdl)
162			pod.handleError(err, "Unable to set the node affinity for the pod.")
163		}
164		if pod.maxRetries() && err != nil {
165			// the number of retries is incremented in the handleError function when the error is not nil.
166			// If we enter this branch, the retries counter has been incremented and reached the max retries.
167			// The counter starts at 1 when the first error occurs. Therefore, when the reconciler tries maxRetries times,
168			// the counter is equal to the maxRetries value and the pod should not be processed again.
169			// Publish this event and remove the scheduling gate.
170			log.Info("Max retries Reached. The pod will not have the nodeAffinity set.")
171			pod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ImageArchitectureInspectionError, fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", ImageInspectionErrorMaxRetriesMsg, err.Error()))
172	
173			if cppc != nil && cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture != "" {
174				log.Info("Setting the nodeAffinity to the fallback architecture", "fallbackArchitecture", cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture)
175				pod.setRequiredNodeAffinityToFallbackArchitecture(cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture)
176			}
177		}
178		// If the pod has been processed successfully or the max retries have been reached, remove the scheduling gate.
179		if err == nil || pod.maxRetries() {
180			// If no preferred node affinity was set by any config, log and publish an event
181			if pod.Labels[utils.PreferredNodeAffinityLabel] == utils.LabelValueNotSet {
182				pod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet,
183					ArchitecturePreferredPredicateSkippedMsg)
184				log.V(2).Info("No preferred node affinity was set")
185			}
186	
187			log.V(1).Info("Removing the scheduling gate from pod.")
188			pod.RemoveSchedulingGate()
189		}
190	}
191	
192	// applyMatchingPPCs applies the pre-filtered matching PodPlacementConfigs to the pod.
193	// The matchingPPCs slice should already be filtered to only include PPCs whose label selector matches the pod.
194	func (r *PodReconciler) applyMatchingPPCs(ctx context.Context, matchingPPCs []multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfig, pod *Pod) {
195		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithName("PodPlacementConfig")
196	
197		// Sort the configurations by descending priority
198		sort.Slice(matchingPPCs, func(i, j int) bool {
199			return matchingPPCs[i].Spec.Priority > matchingPPCs[j].Spec.Priority
200		})
201	
202		// For each matching namespace-scoped configuration, apply if plugin is enabled
203		for _, ppc := range matchingPPCs {
204			log.V(1).Info("Processing PodPlacementConfig", "namespace", ppc.Namespace, "name", ppc.Name)
205	
206			// check if plugin is enabled
207			if !ppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {
208				log.V(1).Info("Skipping PodPlacementConfig NodeAffinityScoring disabled", "namespace", ppc.Namespace, "name", ppc.Name)
209				continue
210			}
211	
212			log.Info("Applying namespace-scoped config", "PodPlacementConfig", ppc.Name)
213			configSource := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigKind, ppc.Name)
214			pod.SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity(ppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, configSource)
215		}
216	}
217	
218	// trackSkippedMatchingConfigs tracks in the annotation which PPC/CPPC configs would have been applied
219	// but were skipped due to user-defined architecture-related preferred affinity.
220	// The matchingPPCs slice should already be filtered to only include PPCs whose label selector matches the pod.
221	func (r *PodReconciler) trackSkippedMatchingConfigs(ctx context.Context, pod *Pod, cppc *multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig, matchingPPCs []multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfig) {
222		// Sort by descending priority (same as applyMatchingPPCs)
223		sort.Slice(matchingPPCs, func(i, j int) bool {
224			return matchingPPCs[i].Spec.Priority > matchingPPCs[j].Spec.Priority
225		})
226	
227		// Track skipped PodPlacementConfigs
228		for _, ppc := range matchingPPCs {
229			if !ppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {
230				continue
231			}
232	
233			configSource := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigKind, ppc.Name)
234			// Track each platform term as skipped
235			for _, platform := range ppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.Platforms {
236				pod.trackAffinitySource(platform.Architecture, platform.Weight, configSource, false)
237			}
238		}
239	
240		// Track skipped ClusterPodPlacementConfig
241		if cppc != nil && cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {
242			for _, platform := range cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.Platforms {
243				pod.trackAffinitySource(platform.Architecture, platform.Weight, multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfigKind, false)
244			}
245		}
246	}
247	
248	// pullSecretDataList returns the list of secrets data for the given pod given its imagePullSecrets field
249	func (r *PodReconciler) pullSecretDataList(ctx context.Context, pod *Pod) ([][]byte, error) {
250		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)
251		secretAuths := make([][]byte, 0)
252		secretList := pod.getPodImagePullSecrets()
253		for _, pullsecret := range secretList {
254			secret, err := r.ClientSet.CoreV1().Secrets(pod.Namespace).Get(ctx, pullsecret, metav1.GetOptions{})
255			if err != nil {
256				log.Error(err, "Error getting secret", "secret", pullsecret)
257				continue
258			}
259			if secretData, err := utils.ExtractAuthFromSecret(secret); err != nil {
260				log.Error(err, "Error extracting auth from secret", "secret", pullsecret)
261				continue
262			} else {
263				secretAuths = append(secretAuths, secretData)
264			}
265		}
266		return secretAuths, nil
267	}
268	
269	// SetupWithManager sets up the controller with the Manager.
270	func (r *PodReconciler) SetupWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {
271		// This reconciler is mostly I/O bound due to the pod and node retrievals, so we can increase the number of concurrent
272		// reconciles to the number of CPUs * 4.
273		// The main bottleneck is the image inspection.
274		maxConcurrentReconciles := runtime2.NumCPU() * 4
275		ctrllog.FromContext(context.Background()).Info("Setting up the PodReconciler with the manager with max"+
276			" concurrent reconciles", "maxConcurrentReconciles", maxConcurrentReconciles)
277	
278		return ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).
279			For(&corev1.Pod{}).
280			// Watch PodPlacementConfig to re-queue gated pods when a PPC is created, updated, or deleted.
281			// Without this, there is a race between a PPC appearing in the API server and the informer
282			// cache syncing it. If a pod is reconciled before the cache has the PPC, the pod is processed
283			// without the PPC's preferred affinity and the scheduling gate is removed — permanently missing
284			// the PPC configuration. By watching PPCs, we re-queue any still-gated pods in the namespace
285			// so they are reprocessed with the PPC now in the cache.
286			//
287			// Limitation: this only helps pods that are still gated (gate=gated) when the PPC watch event
288			// arrives. If a pod's image inspection completes instantly (e.g., cache hit) and the gate is
289			// removed before the PPC watch fires, the pod won't be re-queued. In practice this is rare
290			// because image inspection involves network I/O (hundreds of ms) while informer watches
291			// deliver events in ~10ms. Fully closing this gap would require re-processing ungated pods,
292			// which conflicts with the one-shot scheduling gate design.
293			Watches(
294				&multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfig{},
295				handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(r.mapPPCToPods),
296			).
297			WithOptions(ctrl2.Options{
298				MaxConcurrentReconciles: maxConcurrentReconciles,
299			}).
300			Complete(r)
301	}
302	
303	// mapPPCToPods returns reconcile requests for all gated pods in the PPC's namespace.
304	func (r *PodReconciler) mapPPCToPods(ctx context.Context, obj client.Object) []reconcile.Request {
305		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)
306		podList := &corev1.PodList{}
307		if err := r.List(ctx, podList,
308			client.InNamespace(obj.GetNamespace()),
309			client.MatchingLabels{utils.SchedulingGateLabel: utils.SchedulingGateLabelValueGated},
310		); err != nil {
311			log.Error(err, "Failed to list gated pods for PodPlacementConfig change", "namespace", obj.GetNamespace())
312			return nil
313		}
314		requests := make([]reconcile.Request, 0, len(podList.Items))
315		for i := range podList.Items {
316			requests = append(requests, reconcile.Request{
317				NamespacedName: types.NamespacedName{
318					Name:      podList.Items[i].Name,
319					Namespace: podList.Items[i].Namespace,
320				},
321			})
322		}
323		if len(requests) > 0 {
324			log.V(1).Info("Re-queuing gated pods due to PodPlacementConfig change",
325				"ppc", obj.GetName(), "namespace", obj.GetNamespace(), "podCount", len(requests))
326		}
327		return requests
328	}
329

11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	package podplacement
18	
19	import (
20		"context"
21		"net/http"
22		"sync"
23		"time"
24	
25		corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
26		apierrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors"
27		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
28		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
29		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/json"
30		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
31		"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
32		"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record"
33		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
34		ctrllog "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log"
35		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/webhook/admission"
36	
37		"github.com/panjf2000/ants/v2"
38	
39		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common"
40		multiarchv1beta1 "github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1"
41		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacement/metrics"
42		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/informers/clusterpodplacementconfig"
43		"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils"
44	)
45	
46	// [disabled:operator]kubebuilder:webhook:path=/add-pod-scheduling-gate,mutating=true,sideEffects=None,admissionReviewVersions=v1,failurePolicy=ignore,groups="",resources=pods,verbs=create,versions=v1,name=pod-placement-scheduling-gate.multiarch.openshift.io
47	
48	// PodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook annotates Pods
49	type PodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook struct {
50		client     client.Client
51		clientSet  *kubernetes.Clientset
52		decoder    admission.Decoder
53		once       sync.Once
54		scheme     *runtime.Scheme
55		recorder   record.EventRecorder
56		workerPool *ants.MultiPool
57	}
58	
59	func (a *PodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook) patchedPodResponse(pod *corev1.Pod, req admission.Request) admission.Response {
60		marshaledPod, err := json.Marshal(pod)
61		if err != nil {
62			return admission.Errored(http.StatusInternalServerError, err)
63		}
64		return admission.PatchResponseFromRaw(req.Object.Raw, marshaledPod)
65	}
66	
67	func (a *PodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook) Handle(ctx context.Context, req admission.Request) admission.Response {
68		responseTimeStart := time.Now()
69		defer utils.HistogramObserve(responseTimeStart, metrics.ResponseTime)
70		metrics.ProcessedPodsWH.Inc()
71		a.once.Do(func() {
72			a.decoder = admission.NewDecoder(a.scheme)
73		})
74		pod := newPod(&corev1.Pod{}, ctx, a.recorder)
75	
76		err := a.decoder.Decode(req, &pod.Pod)
77		if err != nil {
78			return admission.Errored(http.StatusBadRequest, err)
79		}
80		log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithValues("namespace", pod.Namespace, "name", pod.Name)
81	
82		cppc := clusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig()
83	
84		// List existing PodPlacementConfigs in the same namespace
85		ppcList := &multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigList{}
86		if err := a.client.List(ctx, ppcList, client.InNamespace(pod.Namespace)); err != nil {
87			log.Error(err, "Failed to list existing PodPlacementConfigs in namespace")
88			// On error, proceed without PPC filtering - fail open
89			ppcList.Items = []multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfig{}
90		}
91	
92		// Filter to only PPCs that match this pod's labels - do this once for efficiency
93		matchingPPCs := pod.filterMatchingPPCs(ppcList)
94	
95		// Set label to indicate if preferred affinity will be set by CPPC or any matching PPC
96		if (cppc != nil && cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName)) ||
97			pod.hasMatchingPPCWithPlugin(matchingPPCs) {
98			pod.EnsureLabel(utils.PreferredNodeAffinityLabel, utils.LabelValueNotSet)
99		}
100		pod.EnsureLabel(utils.NodeAffinityLabel, utils.LabelValueNotSet)
101		pod.EnsureLabel(utils.SchedulingGateLabel, utils.LabelValueNotSet)
102	
103		if pod.shouldIgnorePod(cppc, matchingPPCs) {
104			log.V(3).Info("Ignoring the pod")
105			return a.patchedPodResponse(pod.PodObject(), req)
106		}
107	
108		pod.ensureSchedulingGate()
109		// We also add a label to the pod to indicate that the scheduling gate was added
110		// and this pod expects processing by the operator. That's useful for testing and debugging, but also gives the user
111		// an indication that the pod is waiting for processing and can support kubectl queries to find out which pods are
112		// waiting for processing, for example when the operator is being uninstalled.
113		pod.Labels[utils.SchedulingGateLabel] = utils.SchedulingGateLabelValueGated
114		// we don't care about this goroutine, it's informational,
115		// we know it will finish eventually by design, and we don't need to block the response as we
116		// are right in the admission pipeline, before the pod is persisted.
117		log.V(3).Info("Scheduling gate added to the pod, launching the event creation goroutine")
118		a.delayedSchedulingGatedEvent(ctx, pod.DeepCopy())
119		metrics.GatedPods.Inc()
120		metrics.GatedPodsGauge.Inc()
121		log.V(2).Info("Accepting pod")
122		return a.patchedPodResponse(pod.PodObject(), req)
123	}
124	
125	func (a *PodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook) delayedSchedulingGatedEvent(ctx context.Context, pod *corev1.Pod) {
126		err := a.workerPool.Submit(func() {
127			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 2*time.Minute)
128			defer cancel()
129			log := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithValues("namespace", pod.Namespace, "name", pod.Name,
130				"function", "delayedSchedulingGatedEvent")
131			// We try to get the pod from the API with exponential backoff until we find it or a timeout is reached
132			err := wait.ExponentialBackoff(wait.Backoff{
133				// The maximum time, excluding the time for the execution of the request,
134				// is the sum of a geometric series with factor != 1.
135				// maxTime = duration * (factor^steps - 1) / (factor - 1)
136				// maxTime = 2e-3s * (2^15 - 1) = 65.534s
137				Duration: 2 * time.Millisecond,
138				Factor:   2,
139				Steps:    15,
140			}, func() (bool, error) {
141				createdPod, err := a.clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(pod.Namespace).Get(ctx, pod.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
142				if err == nil {
143					log.V(2).Info("Pod was found", "namespace", pod.Namespace, "name", pod.Name)
144					a.recorder.Event(createdPod, corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareSchedulingGateAdded, SchedulingGateAddedMsg)
145					// Pod was found, return true to stop retrying
146					return true, nil
147				}
148				if apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
149					log.V(3).Info("Pod not found yet", "namespace", pod.Namespace, "name", pod.Name)
150					// Pod not found yet, continue retrying
151					return false, nil
152				}
153				// Stop retrying
154				log.V(3).Info("Failed to get pod", "error", err)
155				return false, err
156			})
157			if err != nil {
158				log.V(2).Info("Failed to get a scheduling gated Pod after retries",
159					"error", err)
160			}
161		})
162		if err != nil {
163			ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithValues("namespace", pod.Namespace, "name", pod.Name,
164				"function", "delayedSchedulingGatedEvent").Error(err, "Failed to submit the delayedSchedulingGatedEvent job")
165		}
166	}
167	
168	func NewPodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook(client client.Client, clientSet *kubernetes.Clientset,
169		scheme *runtime.Scheme, recorder record.EventRecorder, workerPool *ants.MultiPool) *PodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook {
170		a := &PodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook{
171			client:     client,
172			clientSet:  clientSet,
173			scheme:     scheme,
174			recorder:   recorder,
175			workerPool: workerPool,
176		}
177		metrics.InitWebhookMetrics()
178		return a
179	}

30: $(info HOME is not writable, setting it to /tmp/build)
31: HOME := /tmp/build
38:# To re-generate a bundle for any other default channel without changing the default setup, you can:
49:# For example, running 'make bundle-build bundle-push catalog-build catalog-push' will build and push both
54:# You can use it as an arg. (E.g make bundle-build BUNDLE_IMG=<some-registry>/<project-name-bundle>:<tag>)
57:# BUNDLE_GEN_FLAGS are the flags passed to the operator-sdk generate bundle command
68:# Image URL to use all building/pushing image targets
79:# ENVTEST_K8S_VERSION refers to the version of kubebuilder assets to be downloaded by envtest binary.
125:  IMAGE_BUILD_CMD = imagebuilder
128:  IMAGE_BUILD_CMD = $(ENGINE) build
133:all: build
154:.PHONY: manifests
155:manifests: controller-gen ## Generate WebhookConfiguration, ClusterRole and CustomResourceDefinition objects.
156:	$(CONTROLLER_GEN) rbac:roleName=manager-role crd webhook paths="./..." output:crd:artifacts:config=config/crd/bases
158:.PHONY: generate
159:generate: controller-gen ## Generate code containing DeepCopy, DeepCopyInto, and DeepCopyObject method implementations.
161:	$(MAKE) fmt
163:.PHONY: fmt
164:fmt: ## Run go fmt against code.
165:	$(DOCKER_CMD) hack/go-fmt.sh ./
167:.PHONY: vet
168:vet: ## Run go vet against code.
169:	echo "Running go vet..."
170:	$(DOCKER_CMD) go vet ./...
193:test: manifests generate envtest fmt vet goimports gosec lint unit ## Run tests.
198:.PHONY: build
199:build: manifests generate fmt vet ## Build manager binary.
200:	CGO_ENABLED=1 go build -a -o bin/manager cmd/main.go
203:run: manifests generate fmt vet ## Run a controller from your host.
207:# (i.e. docker build --platform linux/arm64 ). However, you must enable docker buildKit for it.
208:# More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/
209:.PHONY: docker-build
210:docker-build: manifests generate ## Build docker image with the manager.
211:	$(ENGINE) build -t ${IMG} --build-arg BUILD_IMAGE=$(BUILD_IMAGE) --build-arg RUNTIME_IMAGE=$(RUNTIME_IMAGE) .
217:# PLATFORMS defines the target platforms for  the manager image be build to provide support to multiple
218:# architectures. (i.e. make docker-buildx IMG=myregistry/mypoperator:0.0.1). To use this option you need to:
219:# - able to use docker buildx . More info: https://docs.docker.com/build/buildx/
220:# - have enable BuildKit, More info: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/build_enhancements/

269:CONTROLLER_GEN ?= $(LOCALBIN)/controller-gen
282:.PHONY: controller-gen
283:controller-gen: $(CONTROLLER_GEN) ## Download controller-gen locally if necessary. If wrong version is installed, it will be overwritten.
284:$(CONTROLLER_GEN): $(LOCALBIN)
285:	test -s $(LOCALBIN)/controller-gen && $(LOCALBIN)/controller-gen --version | grep -q $(CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION) || \
286:	GOBIN=$(LOCALBIN) GOFLAGS='' go install sigs.k8s.io/controller-tools/cmd/controller-gen@$(CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION)
